observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the GSO (a) conduct the required physical count of all
inventory accounts to establish the existence and reliability of the inventory balances
in the financial statement as at year-end; (b) prepare the corresponding RPCI duly
reconciled with the accounting records and submit the same to the Audit Team; and
(c) maintain the required stock cards for each type of supplies and materials pursuant
to Section 119 of the Manual on NGAs for LGUs.

We recommended that the:
a. Inventory Committee (i) conduct complete physical inventory of all PPE of the
Municipality each year and immediately submit the Report on the Physical
Count of PPE (RPCPPE) to the Audit Team; (ii) reconcile the results of physical
count with the accounting records, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006 and
(iii) facilitate the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties to realize
48
income from the sale thereof and to be derecognized from the accounting
records;
b. GSO to prepare the corresponding IIRUP for the reported unserviceable
properties, after which, proceed with the disposal procedures in accordance with
COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020;
c. Accounting Office to submit for audit the supporting documents for JEV 2024-
01001917 dated January 31, 2024 pertinent to the write-off of semi-expendable
properties.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer closely monitor and supervise
all concerned Collecting Officers and ensure that (a) all collections are remitted intact
and regularly at the close of each business day; (b) all collections are deposited intact
on a daily basis or not later than the next banking day; and (c) ensure sufficient
fidelity bond to cover the cash accountabilities of the COs/LO.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. direct the Municipal Accountant to (i) send Demand Letters to existing
cooperatives to settle immediately their loans, which are already due; (ii)
submit the necessary documents to request for authority to write-off the loans
granted to various NGOs;
b. exhaust all possible remedies available to collect from persons liable, such as
resorting to legal remedies, if necessary; and
c. ensure that receivable accounts are properly monitored and appropriately
acted upon so that these are immediately collected or settled when due.

We recommended that Management, through the Human Resource
Management Office: (a) streamline the payroll-related process by addressing any
problems in the procedure to reduce delays in the payroll process particularly,
contract preparation; (b) require the execution of contracts of service prior to the
hiring of job-order and contract of service personnel to legally bind the parties; and
(c) comply with the mandated daily minimum wage at rates prescribed by the
Regional Tripartite Wages and Productivity Board.

We recommended that Management (a) prioritize filling-up the open positions by
hiring new employees, promotion of deserving employees or consider permanent
appointment of casuals or job-order employees; and (b) conduct regular
assessments of staffing needs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, direct the:
a. MDRRM Officer to comply with the submission to the Audit Team of
the LDRRMFIP and the monthly Report on Fund Utilization with
proof of receipt by OCD and the DILG-LGOO; and
b. Municipal Accountant to review the conditions and provisions of the
transfers/grants from the RTNMC/CBNC, and thereafter, prepare a
separate fund utilization report for the fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the contractor/those
responsible persons/parties to repair the defects or refund the amount of repair works or
deduct the amount of the cost deficiencies from any money claim or retention fee
due to the contractor or other securities posted by them.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer
to (i) properly investigate any requests for work suspension of the contractor and
thoroughly ensure that work suspensions and time extensions are authorized only for
valid reasons; (ii) direct the Project Engineer to strictly monitor the status of the infra
projects based on the approved contract duration; and (iii) cause the submission of
the following documents for further review and evaluation:
Construction of Bataraza Municipal Hall
a. PERT/CPM, S-curve or Bar Chart showing the proposed and actual
accomplishments of the project on a monthly basis for the construction of
Bataraza Municipal Hall;
Construction of 320 mtrs. Bataraza Fish Port
b. detailed timeline clearly outlining the sequence of events that justified the full
181-calendar-day time extension during the aftermath of Typhoon Odette;
and detailed timeline clearly demonstrating sequence of events leading to the
necessity for the issuance of TEO Nos. 1, 2 and 3.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer submit a satisfactory
explanation and justification on the discrepancies noted in the Technical Evaluation
Report for further evaluation.

We further recommended that the Municipal Engineer ensure that the
preparation of the Approved Budget for the Contract is compliant with DPWH Order
(D.O.) No. 197 dated October 7, 2016 and such applicable rules and regulations to
avoid any excess on the Contract Cost over COA Estimate.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, being the Chairman of the GFPS, (1)
ensure integration of the GPB with the local legislative budget to ensure that GAD
PPAs are implemented as planned to attain the GAD objectives, and (2) cause the
submission of the GPB and AR to the Audit Team on time.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

Direct the Municipal Accountant and the GSO to (i) update the PPE Ledger Cards and PCs, respectively; (ii) provide adequate description of PPE in their respective reports; and (iii) thereafter, complete the reconciliation of both records for proper verification and validation of existing PPE, taking into consideration the noted deficiencies in the audit;

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to apply the change in accounting policy on depreciation of PPE to reflect the correct carrying amount of affected accounts in the Financial Statements and provide adequate disclosure therefor in the Notes to Financial Statements; and
Require the MENRO to furnish the Municipal Accountant with the Memoranda of Agreement for the recognition of the compost thresher in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has already recognized the compost thresher in the books of accounts.
observations.reason:
PPE ledgers cards and PCs are still not updated and maintained. Likewise, the PPE description was inadequately shown in both records.

The Accounting Office did not make a lapsing schedule to determine the depreciation and carrying amount of PPE. Also, there was no disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) instruct the Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to cause/process the annotation of the TCT of the acquired land and facilitate the transfer of the portion of the subject land in the name of the Municipality to prevent any future land dispute; (b) direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to remit the taxes withheld to the BIR using the appropriate form for capital gains tax; and (c) through the MPDC, submit the lacking supporting documents to substantiate the validity, regularity, and propriety of transactions
observations.reason: Same observation was noted in CY 2021. No annotation was done to the acquired real properties. The Municipal Assessor has not facilitated the transfer of the TCT to the name of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. Direct the MSWDO and MDRRMO to (i) submit the summary report of welfare goods distributed per barangay duly reconciled against the procured goods during the year; and (ii) coordinate with the General Services Office for the submission of monthly report of supplies and materials issued; and (iii) come-up with a master list/database of recipients for easy reference in future distribution of welfare goods; 

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to accordingly recognize delivery and distribution of welfare goods in accordance with IPSAS No. 12 and COA Circular No. 2014-002 based on the actual circumstances as provided by the MDRRMO, MSWDO and GSO; and

Henceforth, direct the MSWDO to handle the receipt, repacking, storage and distribution of welfare goods in accordance with their office mandate and Section 483 of the Local Government Code.
observations.reason:
Reconciliation of distribution list in CY 2020 and monthly report of supplies and materials issued are still not submitted. 

Procurement of goods for distribution was recognized as an outright expense instead of inventory in the books.

The Management has not established guidelines on the manner of distribution of welfare goods. Other offices like MDRRMO still handled the distribution of goods to the constituents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, MENRO and the Municipal Engineer formulate and implement a Sewerage and Septage Management Program not only for the public market but for the whole Bataraza Town Center to prevent discharge of untreated wastewater into the nearby river.
observations.reason: The Management failed to implement the construction of Sewage Treatment Plant at Bataraza Town Center.

observations.recommendation: Based on the foregoing premises, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) through the Municipal Cooperatives Development Officer (i) establish clear-cut guidelines for ABE IBBA program to be adopted in granting loan assistance to cooperatives; (ii) include provision in the NOLA and MOA pertaining to the 25% dole out of the loan amount for the first loan availment, if that is indeed the intention of the Management, and consider including sanctions for non-payment of loans; (iii) compute and impose the penalties on late installment payments; and (iv) reconcile the MCDO records with the Municipal Accountant records to establish the correct outstanding balance of each active cooperative; and (b) obtain authority from the Sangguniang Bayan to ratify and cure the defects on the existing MOA with the respective cooperatives pursuant to Section 22(c) of R.A No. 7160.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) ensure that the parcel/s of land are free from encumbrances prior to acquisition; and (b) through the MPDC and the Municipal Assessor, submit the lacking supporting documents to substantiate the validity, regularity, and propriety of the transactions, to wit:

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Human Resources Management Officer, consider providing additional manpower for the engineering department, subject to availability of funds therefor.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to:
a. conduct periodic inspection of the municipality’s immovable PPE to determine any impairment loss;
b. introduce timely engineering intervention to prolong the serviceability of the assets; and
c. submit inspection report to the Municipal Accountant for recognition and reversal of impairment loss in the books for fair presentation of those assets.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. direct the General Services Officer to (i) submit a budget proposal for the registration and renewal of the insurance coverage of the said PPE and subsequently process the registration and renewal of insurance otherwise he will be liable for the penalties arising therefrom; and (ii) exert efforts to locate files/documents necessary for the registration/renewal of the said PPE, if necessary, contact the property custodians/previous employees concerned;
b. instruct the Municipal Agriculturist to register all agricultural and fishery equipment of the LGU in compliance with R.A. No. 10601 and DA-DILG JMC No. 2018-02; and
c. instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to provide sufficient funds for the: (i) GSO for the registration/renewal of licenses, insurance coverage and provision for other miscellaneous expenses with consideration on sourcing the fund from the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) as provided by Section 21 of R.A. No. 10121; and (ii) MAO for the proper registration of farm inputs as mandated by R.A No. 10601.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the GSO and the Municipal Engineer to reconcile their records on demolished/damaged or self-disposed properties and investigate any negligence on the part of the property custodian; and thereafter forward the said report together with all supporting documents to the auditor, for evaluation.

We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer (i) meticulously prepare and review all budget proposal as to funding source and expenditure programs before its deliberations/enactment by the Legislative Department; (ii) discontinue the practice of borrowing funds from other sources and limit the appropriation of proposed PPAs based on available funds only; and (iii) submit a consolidated report on the status of implementation of each PPA funded by SNW showing all of the reprogram/unexpended MOOE components of the fund as at December 31, 2021 and March 31, 2022 for evaluation.
We also recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan thoroughly review all budget proposal before its enactment for smooth budget execution.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. instruct the Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) to (i) separately show the share from economic zone and its expected utilization in the Annual/Supplemental Budget/s to easily determine the PPAs to be implemented; (ii) reflect the ecozone’s budget in the Statement of Allotment, Obligations and Balances to facilitate its inclusion in the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts; and (iii) submit the details of the funding source and its utilization for CY 2021 for our evaluation;
b. direct the Municipal Accountant to (i) submit a report of utilization of the share from economic zone for CY 2021 and prior years’ remaining fund, if any, as at yearend; and (ii) maintain special account in the general fund for the share from economic zone for reporting and monitoring purposes; and
c. instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to indicate each revenue account in the Statement of Funding Sources (LBP Form No. 08) for budgeting purposes and to facilitate the proper monitoring of fund utilization/disposition.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Acting MBO to provide appropriation for the allowances, incentives and benefits embodied in R.A. No. 7305 and its Revised IRR to all PHWs of the Municipality. We also recommended that the Municipal Vice Mayor ensure the Municipality’s full compliance to R.A. No. 7305 particularly on other additional compensation provided therein.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. stop the practice of granting hospitalization assistance allowance to the employees of the Municipality as this is a duplication of the Universal Health Care Act8;
b. instruct the Human Resources and Management Office to properly review and validate the supporting documents of the employees’ claims for annual mental and medical-physical examination before issuing a certification therefor to ensure that its payment is based on the cost of the actual check-up expenses incurred;
c. direct the Municipal Budget Officer to change the funding source of the annual medical check-up from Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (Donations) to Personal Services (Other Personnel Benefits) subject to the limitations provided by Section 325(a) of R.A. No. 7160; and
d. discontinue the practice of reimbursing medicine expenses like prescription drugs, vitamins, maintenance and similar items, as this is not within the purview of A.O. No. 402 and its IRR.
We also recommended that the Municipal Vice Mayor and the Sangguniang Bayan members:
a. repeal and/or amend all enacted ordinances that are inconsistent with the provisions of A.O. No. 402 and its IRR; and
b. Conduct extensive research and thoroughly discuss during deliberations taking into consideration the provisions of the national laws, its IRR and any amendments thereto before adopting it in the municipal level to forestall deviation/s therefrom and to ensure that enacted ordinances are solid basis of the Executive Department during budget execution and thus guarantee smooth implementation of ordinances.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Budget Officer and the Human Resources Management Officer to submit the identified cost-cutting and systems improvement measures based on each department’s targeted Major Final Outputs (MFOs) that resulted in unencumbered savings as embodied in the CNA; and
b. BAMEA President submit the documents pertaining to the confirmation of the CNAs signed on October 2, 2017 and November 23, 2021.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant formulate clear cut-off policies on month-end closing with checklist of reports needed from the Municipal Treasurer before finalizing the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has already coordinated this matter with the Municipal Treasurer and month-end closing procedures were already established.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to revalidate and verify any third party claims on the road lots before the Municipal Accountant finally recognize the same in the books for better presentation of the Road Network Account balance in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer and the MPDC initially coordinated with the landowners for the possible donation of the ROW.
observations.reason: The road lots are still not recognized in the books as at yearend.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

Cause the refund of the total amount of ₱224,500.00 cash rewards/ incentives given to the municipal and national government personnel without basis;

Instruct the PRAISE Committee to revisit the CSC-approved PRAISE and include therein the guidelines, criteria and nomination or selection procedures for each identified awards and incentives and thereafter submit any amendments to the CSC Regional Office concerned for approval prior to its implementation by the Municipality; and

Henceforth, ensure compliance on the policies on PRAISE set forth in the CSC MC No. 01, s. 2001 and the approved PRAISE guidelines of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction:
The Management noted and complied with the said recommendation.







The Management ensured compliance with the said CSC MC.
observations.reason: ND No. 22-001-100-(20) dated May 5, 2022 in the amount of ₱224,500.00 was received by the persons liable.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: 

MSWDO to (i) submit a written justification on this matter; (ii) submit the billing statements of all the patients showing the deductions signed by the billing clerk and duly received by the patient or next of kin and other supporting documents as provided by DSWD MC No. 11, series of 2019; (iii) revisit the procedures of granting medical assistance and its documentary requirements; (iv) facilitate the issuance of a guarantee letter to the patient duly received by the latter for transparency in the granting of medical assistance; and (v) henceforth, attach all required documents to substantiate the validity of claims; and

Municipal Accountant to install a sound internal control on this type of transaction taking into considerations the procedures and documentary requirements provided by DSWD MC No. 11, series of 2019.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO submitted a written explanation on this matter.
observations.reason:
The billing statements duly acknowledged by the patients are not yet submitted to date. Disbursement vouchers were still not supported with complete documentation.

Disbursement vouchers were still processed in CY 2021 despite of incomplete supporting documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer, GSO, Municipal Accountant and BAC to (a) formulate a coordinated process/ procedures on requisition, delivery, inspection and issuance of supplies and equipment; and (b) review and identify the controls to be implemented taking into consideration the mentioned lapses in the internal control procedures laid down in Chapter 8 of the Handbook on PSMS.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant upon receipt of the Disbursement Vouchers for processing, to reject/return the incompletely filled out forms attached thereto to compel the concerned responsible officers’ compliance with the handbook on PSMS, as part of the internal audit service of the Municipality pursuant to Section 474(b) of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction:
The Management has taken corrective actions in the processing of PRs, POs and IARs.




The Municipal Accountant imposed strict measures to comply with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) direct the Municipal Engineer and the MENRO to prioritize the completion of the SLF; (b) through the MENRO, submit the revised SWMP inclusive of the actual structures already constructed and the timeline for the completion of the SLF; and (c) dispose the segregated recyclable wastes to make room for incoming wastes.
observations.managementAction: The Management complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor prioritize the construction of new slaughterhouse and fill-up the position of Meat Inspector I to strengthen the basic meat inspection services provided by the Municipality and assure the public that meat products offered for sale in the market are fit for human consumption
observations.managementAction: A Meat Inspector was hired starting March 2021.
observations.reason: As to the funding of the slaughterhouse, the Management is still waiting for the approval and counterpart of the National Meat Inspection Service (NMIS) for its construction.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to cause/process the annotation of the TCT of the acquired land and facilitate the transfer of the portion thereof in the name of the Municipality to prevent any future land dispute.
observations.reason:
The Municipal Assessor and the MPDC was not able to process the annotation of those real properties.

Refer to Observation No. 4, Part II hereof.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the MENRO (a) assign a permanent equipment in the facility to undertake daily soil cover on collected residual wastes; and (b) fast track the completion of the expansion area of the SLF.
observations.reason:
No permanent equipment assigned in the facility to undertake daily soil cover.

The SLF was not yet completed. Refer to Observation No. 8, Part II.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, MENRO and the Municipal Engineer formulate and implement a Sewerage and Septage Management Program not only for the public market but for the whole Bataraza Town Center to prevent discharge of untreated wastewater into the nearby river.
observations.reason:
The Management failed to implement a sewage and septage management program in the Bataraza Town Center in CY 2020.

An amount of ₱12,000,000.00 was included in CY 2021 budget for the construction of Sewage Treatment Plant at Bataraza Town Center.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor prioritize the construction of new slaughterhouse and fill-up the position of Meat Inspector I to strengthen the basic meat inspection services provided by the Municipality and assure the public that meat products offered for sale in the market are fit for human consumption.
observations.reason:
The Municipality has included in its AIP of CY 2021 for the construction of the New slaughterhouse amounting to ₱30,000,000.00.

The Meat Inspector was filled-in only in March 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant formulate clear cut-off policies on month-end closing with checklist of reports needed from the Municipal Treasurer before finalizing the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Direct the Municipal Accountant and the GSO to (i) update the PPE Ledger Cards and PCs, respectively; (ii) provide adequate description of PPE in their respective reports; and (iii) thereafter, complete the reconciliation of both records for proper verification and validation of existing PPE, taking into consideration the noted deficiencies in the audit;
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to apply the change in accounting policy on depreciation of PPE to reflect the correct carrying amount of affected accounts in the Financial Statements and provide adequate disclosure therefor in the Notes to Financial Statements; and
Require the MENRO to furnish the Municipal Accountant with the Memoranda of Agreement for the recognition of the compost thresher in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to revalidate and verify any third party claims on the road lots before the Municipal Accountant finally recognize the same in the books for better presentation of the Road Network Account balance in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) instruct the Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to cause/process the annotation of the TCT of the acquired land and facilitate the transfer of the portion of the subject land in the name of the Municipality to prevent any future land dispute; (b) direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to remit the taxes withheld to the BIR using the appropriate form for capital gains tax; and (c) through the MPDC, submit the lacking supporting documents to substantiate the validity, regularity, and propriety of transactions, to wit:
TCT/TD No. Supporting Documents
TD No. 18-005-001-5526 o Certified Photocopy of Tax Clearance from Assessor’s Office
TD No. 18-005-001-5528 where the land is located
TD No. 18-005-001-5529 - Certificate Authorizing registration (CAR)
TD No. 18-005-001-5531 - Capital Gains Tax (CGT)
TD No. 18-005-001-5547 - Documentary Stamp Tax (DST)
- Transfer Tax
o If unregistered land:
- Certified photocopy of TD in the name of the
procuring entity or previous owner with annotation
of sale 
- Notarized Affidavit of Ownership 
- Certification from the City/Municipal Treasurer that
the claimant is the sole entity paying the taxes for
the property for the past five years
- Notarized joint affidavit of two adjoining landowners
or Barangay/Local Officials concerned attesting to
the ownership of land
- If claim for payment or amount of sale exceeds
₱50,000 (EO No. 1035), certified photocopy of
Claimant's Surety/Indemnity Bond issued by any
Insurance Company duly accredited by the Insurance
Commission
TCT No. T-19642 o Certified Photocopy of Tax Clearance from Assessor’s Office
where the land is located
- Certificate Authorizing registration (CAR)
- Capital Gains Tax (CGT)
- Documentary Stamp Tax (DST)
- Transfer Tax
o If registered land:
- If portion of lot is acquired, certified photocopy of
TCT and TD in the name of the procuring entity or
previous owner with annotation of sale

The Municipal Assessor will initiate the process of annotation of the TCT of the
acquired land in the name of the municipality after fund is allotted for that purpose.

The Municipal Accountant will release the payment to the BIR regarding the withholding of capital gains tax from the seller of the land/lot once the Municipal Assessor has notified and submitted the BIR Statement of Billing to her.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Cause the refund of the total amount of ₱224,500.00 cash rewards/ incentives given to the municipal and national government personnel without basis;
Instruct the PRAISE Committee to revisit the CSC-approved PRAISE and include therein the guidelines, criteria and nomination or selection procedures for each identified awards and incentives and thereafter submit any amendments to the CSC Regional Office concerned for approval prior to its implementation by the Municipality; and
Henceforth, ensure compliance on the policies on PRAISE set forth in the CSC MC No. 01, s. 2001 and the approved PRAISE guidelines of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
MSWDO to (i) submit a written justification on this matter; (ii) submit the billing statements of all the patients showing the deductions signed by the billing clerk and duly received by the patient or next of kin and other supporting documents as provided by DSWD MC No. 11, series of 2019; (iii) revisit the procedures of granting medical assistance and its documentary requirements; (iv) facilitate the issuance of a guarantee letter to the patient duly received by the latter for transparency in the granting of medical assistance; and (v) henceforth, attach all required documents to substantiate the validity of claims; and
Municipal Accountant to install a sound internal control on this type of transaction taking into considerations the procedures and documentary requirements provided by DSWD MC No. 11, series of 2019.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer, GSO, Municipal Accountant and BAC to (a) formulate a coordinated process/procedures on requisition, delivery, inspection and issuance of supplies and equipment; and (b) review and identify the controls to be implemented taking into consideration the mentioned lapses in the internal control procedures laid down in Chapter 8 of the Handbook on PSMS.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant upon receipt of the Disbursement Vouchers for processing, to reject/return the incompletely filled out forms attached thereto to compel the concerned responsible officers’ compliance with the handbook on PSMS, as part of the internal audit service of the Municipality pursuant to Section 474(b) of RA No. 7160.

observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Mayor designated Ms. Angela R. Ustares as SDO who will handle disbursements for special purpose activities of the LGU, effective on June 17, 2020. Likewise, Ms. Shiela Marie Dumara-og and Ms. Virginia Palanca were designated on January 7, 2020 as SDOs under the Mayor’s Office. 

Since there are three SDOs, transfer of cash advance from one accountable officer (AO) to another AO was stopped.

A copy of the subsidiary ledger showing the details of partial payments made by Ms. Laxamana, was submitted to the Audit Team to support the amount reported on the Status of Unliquidated Cash Advances as at December 31, 2019.
observations.reason: The Management will communicate and confer to the families of the concerned former Municipal Treasurers the necessary documents required for the request to write off dormant accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the (a) Inventory Committee to complete the physical count of remaining PPE; and (b) Municipal Accountant and the Municipal GSO to (i) update the PPELCs and PCs, respectively, (ii) provide adequate description of PPE in their respective reports and (iii) complete the reconciliation of both records for proper verification and validation of existing PPE, taking into consideration the noted deficiencies in the audit.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) compute the Accumulated Depreciation for the PPE listed in Annex 5; (b) identify the amount of the PPE that was recorded in the books but failed to indicate the amount; and (c) maintain a RPLC indicating therein the basic information about the real properties owned by the Municipality.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to revalidate and verify any third party claims on the road lots before the Municipal Accountant finally recognize the same in the books for fair presentation of the Road Network Account balance in the financial statements.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor submit an explanation on the noted deficiencies in the disposal of government property. Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor comply with the guidelines in the disposal of government property by destruction.
observations.managementAction: The physical inventory count was completed.
observations.reason:
The reconciliation of Accounting and GSO records was not completed in CY 2020.

There was no report prepared and submitted to COA on the deficiencies regarding the disposal of the slaughterhouse thru destruction.

Refer to Observation No. 2, Part II hereof.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) identify the Cost of the PPE classified as Donation/Others in the Sale of Disposed PPE and the computation of the accumulated depreciation and thereafter, submit a report/the information for our reference in audit; and (b) draw the necessary journal entries to adjust the Other PPE and its Accumulated Depreciation account balances.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made the adjusting entries in the books in July 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary journal entries this year 2020 to adjust the affected accounts for fair presentation of the Road Networks and Government Equity account balances.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made the adjusting entries in the books in July 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant submit the details of the share from national wealth’s budget and its utilization for CY 2019 and prior years’ continuing appropriations.

We reiterated the prior years’ recommendation that the Municipal Budget Officer (a) separately show the estimated share from national wealth and its estimated utilization in the Annual Budget to easily identify the PPAs to be implemented; and (b) ensure that any discontinued PPAs and unexpended balances out of that fund are reprogrammed in accordance with Section 294 of the LGC to be reflected separately in the SAAOB.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant record, present and report the shares from national wealth and for economic zones as specials accounts in the General Fund and maintain subsidiary ledger for each PPAs in accordance with Sections 105 and 107 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Budget Officer submitted the revised reports on the details of the share from national wealth’s budget and its utilization for CYs 2018 and 2019 on July 15, 2020.

The PPAs under the share from national wealth was separately shown in the Annual Budget as recommended.

In the Municipal Accountant record, it presented and reported the shares from national wealth and for economic zones as specials accounts in the General Fund and maintain subsidiary ledger for each PPAs in accordance with Sections 105 and 107 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) require the General Services Officer and the BAC to comply with the provision of the 2016 R-IRR of R.A. No. 9184 concerning procurement of CSE; and (b) instruct the BAC to consolidate all CSE purchase requests of all departments as shown in the Annual Procurement Plan and utilize the functionality of the mPhilGEPS in ordering CSE through the Virtual Store.
observations.managementAction: The Management complied with provision of the 2016 R-IRR of R.A. No. 9184 concerning procurement of CSE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer to submit immediately to the Audit Team the monitoring reports on the implementation of government PPAs for CY 2019 in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.
observations.managementAction: The Management submitted the Monitoring Report on Government PPAs as at December 31, 2019 on July 14, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create and fill-up the three (3) statutory positions of the DRRM Office in compliance with Section 12 of R.A. No. 10121 to enhance its capacity and ensure efficient and effective delivery of services in time of calamities.
observations.managementAction: Three statutory positions of the DRRM Office were created but included in the Annual Budget of CY 2021.
observations.reason: The positions were not yet filled-up.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

a. Municipal Mayor (i) designate an SDO, different from the DO, who will handle
disbursements for special purpose/time-bound activities or events; (ii) limit the
grant of cash advances within the authorized maximum cash accountability
covered by the approved amount of bond of the DO; and (iii) file request for
authority to write-off dormant accounts from COA duly supported with necessary
papers/documents as embodied in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19,
2016;
b. DO/SDO refrain from transferring his cash advance or accountability to another
accountable officer;
c. Municipal Accountant compute the total amount deducted from the salaries of Ms.
Aurora Laxamana, Local Revenue Collecting Officer I, and serve a written notice
demanding immediate settlement of her outstanding cash advances as mandated in
COA Circular No. 2012-004, otherwise, cause the suspension/withholding of her
salary upon failure to settle her accountabilities; and
d. Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Accountant strictly observe the provisions of
COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1992 on the proper granting,
utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the (a) Inventory
Committee to complete the physical count of remaining PPE; and (b) Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal GSO to (i) update the PPE ledger cards (PPELCs) and
property cards (PCs), respectively, (ii) provide adequate description of PPE in their
respective reports and (iii) complete the reconciliation of both records for proper
verification and validation of existing PPE, taking into consideration the noted
deficiencies in the audit.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) compute the
Accumulated Depreciation for the PPE listed in Annex 5; (b) identify the amount of
the PPE that was recorded in the books but failed to indicate the amount; and (c)
maintain the real property ledger cards (RPLCs) indicating therein the basic
information about the real properties owned by the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor and the
Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to revalidate and verify any third
party claims on the road lots before the Municipal Accountant finally recognize the
same in the books for fair presentation of the Road Network account balance in the
financial statements.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor submit an explanation on the noted
deficiencies in the disposal of government property. Henceforth, we recommended
that the Municipal Mayor comply with the guidelines on the disposal of government
property by destruction.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) identify the Cost of the
PPE classified as Donation/Others in the Sale of Disposed PPE and the
computation of the accumulated depreciation and thereafter, submit a report/the
information for our reference in audit; and (b) draw the necessary journal entries
to adjust the Other PPE and its Accumulated Depreciation account balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary
journal entries this year 2020 to adjust the affected accounts for fair presentation
of the Road Networks and Government Equity account balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor
and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to cause/process the
annotation of the TCT of the acquired land and facilitate the transfer of the
portion thereof in the name of the Municipality to prevent any future land dispute.

We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal
Accountant submit the details of the share from national wealth’s budget and its
utilization for CY 2019 and prior years’ continuing appropriations.

We reiterated the prior years’ recommendation that the Municipal Budget
Officer (a) separately show the share from national wealth’s budget and its
utilization in the Annual Budget to easily identify the PPAs to be implemented; and
(b) ensure that any discontinued PPAs and unexpended balances out of that fund are
reprogrammed in accordance with Section 294 of the LGC and reflected separately
in the Statement of Appropriation, Allotment, Obligations and Balances.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant record, present and report the
shares from national wealth and for economic zones as specials accounts in the
General Fund and maintain subsidiary ledger for each PPAs in accordance with
Sections 105 and 107 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) require the General
Services Officer and the BAC to comply with the provision of the 2016 R-IRR of
R.A. No. 9184 concerning procurement of CSE; and (b) instruct the BAC to
consolidate all CSE purchase requests of all departments as shown in the Annual
Procurement Plan and utilize the functionality of the mPhilGEPS in ordering CSE
through the Virtual Store.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer to submit
immediately to the Audit Team the monitoring reports on the implementation of
government PPAs for CY 2019 in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular
No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: 1. Municipal Accountant to exert diligent effort in reviewing and analyzing record reconciling items and adjustments; 2. Municipal Treasurer to communicate with the Municipal Accountant whenever there are transactions that warrant entries in the books of accounts; and 3. Municipal Treasurer to notify Ms. Sheila Marie Dumara-og to return the stale check in her possession for replacement. Otherwise, take up in the books of accounts the amount of the stale check as surplus adjustment. Henceforth, instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to conduct briefing to newly assumed/designated accountable officers on the proper discharge of their duties and other matters related to their work before they assume their posts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant assigned a technical staff to review and analyze records for reconciliation and outstanding book reconciling items and adjustments. The Municipal Treasurer always informed the Municipal Accountant of the transactions and conducted counterchecking of records/ entries in the books of accounts. Ms. Dumara-og was notified and searched for stale check but to no avail. She decided to just take in the book of accounts as surplus adjustment. Henceforth, instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to conduct briefing to newly assumed/designated accountable officers on the proper discharge of their duties and other matters related to their work before they assume their posts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant reconcile their records in accordance with Section 63(f) of LTOM and Section 181 (c) of GAAM Vol. I so that errors or discrepancies noted are adjusted accordingly.
observations.managementAction: The records of both the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant were reconciled as at December 31, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendations that the Management through the Municipal Accountant: a. Issue demand letters to concerned Accountable Officers who are still in the service for the immediate settlement/liquidation of their accounts, otherwise, cause the withholding of their salaries to enforce liquidation of their accountabilities; b. Send notice to the former Municipal Treasurer to settle their accountabilities otherwise, cause the filing of appropriate charges against them; and c. Strictly observe the rules and regulations on the proper granting, utilization and liquidation of all cash advances pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1992.
observations.managementAction: (a) sent Demand Letters to all concerned accountable officers; and (b) assigned a staff to monitor and follow up liquidation of cash advances of officials and employees.
observations.reason: Refer to Observation No. 1, Part II hereof.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the (a) Municipal Accountant and the Municipal GSO to update the PPELCs and PCs, respectively, and reconcile both records for proper verification and validation of existing PPE, taking into consideration the noted deficiencies in the audit; (b) require the Inventory Committee/Municipal GSO to present the RPCPPE by type of properties, account the unit cost/fair value of the other PPE items identified in the RPCPPE, and ensure that all properties owned by the agency are labeled or assigned with specific Property Number for proper identification; and (c) direct the Accountant to draw the necessary journal entries to adjust the derecognized Other PPE account and overstated Impairment Loss.
observations.managementAction: Not Refer to
observations.reason: Refer to Observation No. 2, Part II hereof.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan strictly adhere to Sections 289 and 294 of the LGC on the utilization of the fund. We recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant submit the details of the share from national wealth’s budget and its utilization for CY 2018. We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer (a) separately show the share from national wealth’s budget and its utilization in the Annual Budget to easily identify the PPAs to be implemented, and (b) ensure that any discontinued and unexpended balances out of that fund are reprogrammed in accordance with Section 294 of the LGC and reflected separately in the SAAOB.
observations.managementAction: Not Refer to
observations.reason: Refer to Observation No. 5, Part II hereof.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record, present and report the shares from national wealth and for economic zones as special accounts in the General Fund and maintain subsidiary ledger for each plans, programs and activities (PPAs) in accordance with Sections 105 and 107, Chapter 6, Volume 1 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: Not Refer to
observations.reason: Refer to Observation No. 5, Part II hereof.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) require immediate submission of medical results, official receipts, copies of medical results and other documents evidencing payment of hospitalization or annual check-up in CY 2018 of the concerned officials and employees. Any amount not actually expended should be immediately refunded; (b) stop the grant of medical allowance to municipal officials and employees; and (c) henceforth, strictly observe the provision of CSC Memorandum Circular No. 33, s. 1997 and Administrative Order No. 402, s. 1998 in providing health program for local employees.
observations.managementAction: The Management required immediate submission of medical results, official receipts, copies of medical results and other documents evidencing payment of hospitalization or annual check-up in CY 2018 of the concerned officials and employees. Any amount not actually expended should be immediately refunded; (b) stop the grant of medical allowance to municipal officials and employees; and (c) henceforth, strictly observe the provision of CSC Memorandum Circular No. 33, s. 1997 and Administrative Order No. 402, s. 1998 in providing health program for local employees.

observations.recommendation: We therefore recommended that the Municipal Mayor stop the granting of retirement additional retirement benefit without legal basis and demand refund of the retirement award amounting to P181,240.00 because to allow the payees to retain the cash retirement award privilege would amount to their unjust enrichment to the prejudice of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Management has stopped the granting of additional retirement benefit without legal basis and demand refund of the retirement award amounting to P181,240.00. The Management provided the necessary actions to recover the amount.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC Chairman, Municipal Accountant and the Municipal GSO submit satisfactory explanations on the noted observations and submit all the lacking documents to establish the propriety of the procurements. Hereafter, ensure that (a) all the documentary requirements are complete before payment of claims; (b) warranty security is imposed to assure that manufacturing defects will be corrected; (c) all required details in the PO are accurately itemized as to its detailed specification; and (d) inspection of delivered items is conducted.
observations.managementAction: The Management provided the lacking documents and ensured the completeness of documentary requirements before payment of claims. Moreover, the Management implemented the following practices: a. Warranty security is now being required from the suppliers. b. Items in the PO are accurately itemized to its detailed specification. c. Inspection of delivered items is now being conducted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant include a separate discussion on the details of the Trust Liability — DRRM account in the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 in order to present a more comprehensive information on the account.
observations.managementAction: Disclosure on Trust Liability – DRRM was already provided in the Notes to Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) Chairperson to strictly monitor the implementation of GAD PPAs and conduct regular coordination with the implementing office/s to ensure that plans are undertaken within the target period.
observations.managementAction: The Management gathered the remaining 313 GAD indicators from the concerned agencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, being the GFPS chairperson, facilitate the updating of its existing GAD database to have an effective basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation and to undertake thorough gender analysis based on relevant data for thus, proper identification of existing gender issues to ensure that the approved PPAs are responsive to address the same.
observations.managementAction: The Management gathered the remaining GAD indicators from the concerned agencies.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
1. Municipal Accountant to exert diligent effort in reviewing and analyzing
outstanding book reconciling items and record adjustments with proper
supporting documentation;
2. Municipal Treasurer to communicate with the Municipal Accountant
whenever there are transactions that warrant corresponding entries in the
books of accounts; and
3. Municipal Treasurer to notify Ms. Sheila Marie Dumara-og to return the stale
check in her possession for replacement. Otherwise, take up in the books of
accounts the amount of the stale check as surplus adjustment pursuant to
Section 97 of P.D. 1445;

Henceforth, instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer
to conduct briefing to newly assumed/designated accountable officers on the proper
discharge of their duties and other matters related to their work before they assume
their posts.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal
Accountant reconcile their records in accordance with Section 63(f) of LTOM and
Section 181 (c) of GAAM Vol. I so that errors or discrepancies noted are adjusted
accordingly.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Management through the
Municipal Accountant:
a. Issue demand letters to concerned Accountable Officers who are still in the
service for the immediate settlement/liquidation of their accounts,
otherwise, cause the withholding of their salaries to enforce liquidation of
their accountabilities;
b. Send notice to the former Municipal Treasurer to settle their
accountabilities otherwise, cause the filing of appropriate charges against
them; and
c. Strictly observe the rules and regulations on the proper granting,
utilization and liquidation of all cash advances pursuant to COA Circular No.
97-002 dated February 10, 1992.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal GSO to update PPELCs
and PCs, respectively, and reconcile both records for proper verification and
validation of existing PPE, taking into consideration the noted deficiencies in
audit;
b. require the Inventory Committee/Municipal GSO to present the RPCPPE by
type of properties, account the unit cost/fair value of the PPE items identified
in the RPCPPE, and ensure that all properties owned by the agency are
properly labeled or assigned with specific Property Number for proper
identification of the PPE; and
c. direct the Accountant to draw the necessary journal entries to adjust the
derecognized Other PPE account and overstated Impairment Loss.

We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan strictly adhere to Sections
289 and 294 of the LGC on the utilization of the fund. We recommend that the
Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant submit the details of the
share from national wealth’s budget and its utilization for CY 2018.

We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer (a) separately
show the share from national wealth’s budget and its utilization in the Annual
Budget to easily identify the PPAs to be implemented, and (b) ensure that any
discontinued and unexpended balances out of that fund are reprogrammed in
accordance with Section 294 of the LGC and reflected separately in the SAAOB.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record, present and
report the shares from national wealth and for economic zones as special accounts
in the General Fund and maintain subsidiary ledger for each plans, programs and
activities (PPAs) in accordance with Sections 105 and 107, Chapter 6, Volume 1
of the National Government Accounting System (NGAS) for local government
units.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) require the immediate
submission of official receipts, copies of medical results and certificates and other
documents evidencing payment of hospitalization or annual check-up in CY 2018
of the concerned officials and employees. Any amount not actually expended
should be immediately refunded; (b) stop the grant of medical allowance to
municipal officials and employees; and (c) henceforth, strictly observe the
provision of CSC Memorandum Circular No. 33, s. 1997 and Administrative
Order No. 402, s. 1998 in providing health program for local employees.

We therefore recommended that the Municipal Mayor stop the granting of
retirement award/privileges without legal basis and demand refund of the
retirement award amounting to P181,240.00 because to allow the payees to retain
the cash retirement award/privilege would amount to their unjust enrichment to
the prejudice of the Municipality.
Insure all government properties with the Property Insurance Fund of the GSIS.
observations.managementAction: Not Still researching files for some vehicles.
observations.reason: Still waiting for the technical plans and cost estimates from the office of the Municipal Engineer.

observations.recommendation: a. Management to comply with Section 12 of R.A. No. 10121 and include the LDRRMO in the process/flow of documents involving the utilization of the LDRRMF. Management should strictly adhere to the rules and regulations in the passage of that fund’s annual budget and plan as laid down by that Act. b. The LDRRMO to submit the operational highlights of the fund containing the planned activities vis-a-vis the accomplishments and its corresponding cost, the list of DRRM Equipment and inventory of supplies as well as implemented, on-going and completed projects of the LDRRMO. c. Management strictly utilize the LDRRMF solely for disaster risk reduction and management activities pursuant to R.A. No. 10121 and DILG Memorandum Circular, No. 2012-73 dated April 17, 2012. For this purpose, unnecessary expenditures should be avoided to ensure the availability of resources in mitigating the effects of calamities.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMO be required to submit monthly the Report on Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund and certified correct by the Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: Some items were: submit the planned activities and its costs; Still to be presented to the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation in the CY 2016 Annual
Report that the Assets Inventory Committee should fast track the completion
of the reconciliation of the physical inventory count and acconnting records and
furnish the Audit Team with the said report. The Municipal Mayor should assign additional manpower to the
General Services Office and the Municipal Accountant’s Office to expedite the
reconciliation process, undergo the actual physical count, maintain inventory cards for
each item of PPE, prepare the required annual physical inventory report and monitor
the same.

We recommended the GSO to maintain property cards and establish a
database of all PPEs of the Municipality to facilitate the reconciliation of the
same to the physical inventory count upon its completion.

We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to compel the real property
owners to pay the undercollection, otherwise she will be held personally liable.
The Municipal Treasurer should direct all collectors to strictly comply with
the provisions of the LGC on granting discount to real property owners.

We recommended the Sangguniang Bayan to revisit and revise the Revenue Code
to comply with the provisions of the LGC and LTOM.

We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to update all the collectors with
emphasis on their personal liability in case of understatement of collections
imposed upon the taxpayer’s payments.

b. Imposition of discounts and penalties on real property taxes were not
applied uniformly to all taxpayers in violation of Sections 251 and 255 of
Republic Act No. 7160. Likewise, payment for prior year’s taxes were given
discounts instead of penalties.

The pertinent sections of R.A. 7160 otherwise known as the Local
Government Code states that:

SECTION 251. Tax Discount for Advanced Prompt Payment. - [f
the basic real property tax and the additional tax accruing to the
Special Education Fund (SEF) are paid in advance in accordance
with the prescribed schedule of payment as provided under Section
250, the Sanggunian concerned may grant a discount not exceeding
twenty percent (20%) of the annual tax due.

SECTION 255. Interests on Unpaid Real Property Tax. - In case of
Jailure to pay the basic real property tax or any other tax levied under
this Title upon the expiration of the periods as provided in Section
250, or when due, as the case may be, shall subject the taxpayer to the
payment of interest at the rate of two percent (2%) per month on the
unpaid amount or a fraction thereof, until the delinquent tax shall
have been fully paid: Provided, however, That in no case shall the
total interest on the unpaid tax or portion thereof exceed thirty-six
(36) months.

Review of the Real Property Tax Abstract of Collection disclosed that

discounts and penalties were not uniformly imposed to all taxpayers in violation of
the foregoing provisions.

Further, analysis of the abstract of payments disciosed that some of the
collections were not turn over daily or weekly, in the case of field collectors, to the
Liquidating Officer. Some of the Collectors took years to remit their collections to
the Liquidating Officer, contrary to Section 60 of the LTOM which states that:

A. Daily Turnover of Collections. At the close of the business
day, Collectors/Tellers shall turn over all their collections intact (o
the Local Treasurer/Cashier. Xxx.

In the case of collectors assigned to the field, where travel time from
their places of assignment to the Local Treasury Office is more than
one (1) day, turn over of collections shall be made at least once a
week or as soon as the collections reach P3,000.00.

For properties subject to back taxes, some taxpayers paid for the penalties
while others do not. Likewise, payments of prior year’s taxes were given 10% to
20% discounts instead of imposing penalties on those propertics, thus, resuited to
the understatement of collections.

We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to update all the collectors
with emphasis on their personal liability in case of understatement of
collections imposed upon the taxpayer’s payments and comply with Sections
251 and 255 of R.A. No. 7160.

We recommended the Management to:

d. Use the PS appropriations for compensation and benefits of regular and
permanent employees;

e. Fund and charge the salaries and wages of COS/JO against the MOOE in
the approved budget; and

f. Fill up the vacant positions and the COS/JO personnel, who meet the
appropriate eligibility and qualification requirements, shall be given
priority in the hiring by the Municipality.

We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to send a written notice to
the concerned payees at least one month before a check becomes stale.
Moreover, we require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary
adjustments in the books for the long outstanding/stale checks in accordance
with Section 59, NGAS Manual, Volume 1.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Treasurer to
close the Municipality’s account with FCB. We recommend the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to prepare the Bank Reconciliation
Statements for that account.

We recommended the Sangguniang Bayan to strictly adhere to Section
294 of the LGC on the utilization of the fund. We recommend the Municipal
Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to submit the details of the share
from national wealth’s budget and its utilization for CYs 2016 and 2017 for
our evaluation.

We recommended the Municipal Budget Officer to separately show the share from
national wealth’s budget and its utilization in the Annual Budget to easily identified
the PPAs to be implemented. Any discontinued and unexpended balances out of
that fund should be reprogrammed in accordance with Section 294 of the LGC and
reflected separately in the SAAOB.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to require the accounting
and budget offices to reconcile their records on allotments and obligations
monthly and prepare and submit the required reconciliation report at year-
end. We further recommend that the list of reconciling items be submitted to
supplement the report.

We recommended that the BAC and the BAC Secretariat strictly abide
by the posting requirements in the PhilGEPS of all procurement
opportunities, notices, awards and other related information, in compliance
with Sections 8.2.1(a), 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the 2016 Revised LR.R. of R.A. No.
9184.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to ensure that the required
reports on projects/programs/activities are submitted within the period
prescribed in COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 as amended
by COA Circular No. 2015-006 dated August 20, 2015. The monitoring report
on the Municipality’s projects/programs/activities shouid contain details as to
project name, location, total cost, date started, extensions, target and actual
completion date, project status and total cost incurred to date duly reconciled
with the accounting records.

We recommended for the BAC to prepare the PMR in its prescribed
format after the end of each semester for the approval and submission by the
Municipal Mayor to the GPPB, in compliance with Section 12.2 of the IRR of
RA No. 9184.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal
Treasurer to stamp “PAID” all the disbursement vouchers and its supporting
documents in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 92-389.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Assets Inventory Committee should fast track the completion of the reconciliation of the physical inventory count and accounting records and furnish the Audit Team with the said report. The Municipal Mayor should assign additional manpower to the General Services Officer’s Office to expedite the reconciliation process, undergo the actual physical count, maintain inventory cards for each item of PPE, prepare the required annual physical inventory report and monitor the same.
observations.managementAction: Hire additional manpower to be assigned to GSO. Maintain inventory cards for each PPE item. Prepare Annual Physical Inventory Report. Personnel was not present/ available during the schedules visit of the Inventory Team.
observations.reason: Some items were: Still to be presented to the Inventory Committee; Accountable Personnel was not present/ available during the schedules visit of the Inventory Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the GSO to maintain property cards and establish a database of all PPEs of the Municipality to facilitate the reconciliation of the same to the physical inventory count upon its completion.

Require all employees/officials to liquidate advances before year-end. We recommended the Municipal Accountant to send demand letters to the officials and employees with unliquidated accounts and cause the withholding of their respective salaries and see to it that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of each year.
observations.managementAction: Maintain books for all cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Chief Executive the institutionalization of GAD in the Municipal and the integration thereof to its overall annual plans and programs and strict adherence to the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 and to submit the required reports to the concerned national government agencies. Formulate Gender and Development (GAD) Plan that would reflect the actual gender issues and concerns prevailing in the locality. The GAD programs shall be implemented consistent to the duly formulated and funded approved GAD plan by the DILG. Identify appropriate PPAs to address priority gender issues to be included in the GPB.
observations.managementAction: Institutionalize GAD in Municipality, submit Annual GAD plans and programs to concerned agencies.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee headed by Municipal GSO that will be responsible for the conduct of the annual physical inventory count, reconcile the result of the inventory taking with the property records and furnish the Audit Team with the required RPCPPE.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant submit for audit the pertinent supporting documents for ₱11,701,627.21 to warrant the adjustments made in the Other Assets account last year.

The Municipal Accountant should reclassify the software from PPE account to Intangible Assets account and determine the related amortization or any impairment in its cost to reflect its carrying amount, if applicable.

We recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board (MSWMB), require the closure of the open dump site at Purok Tagumpay, Bgy. Inogbong and all other existing open dump sites within the jurisdiction of the Municipality and establish the operation of a sanitary landfill in accordance with Section 40 and 41 of RA 9003. Solid Waste Management funds should be appropriated for related programs and activities prescribed by RA 9003. The 10-year Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan should likewise be prepared by the MSWMB consistent with the provisions in Section 16 and 17 of RA 9003 and duly approved by the National Solid Waste Management Commission.

We therefore recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer and the MPDC to immediately submit the list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities (PPAs) for CY 2016 of the Municipality and those that are to be implemented during the year as well as the monthly monitoring report on implementation of PPAs containing details as to project name, location, total cost, date started, extensions, target and actual completion date, project status and total cost incurred to date.

Henceforth, we recommended strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2010-101 on the information and publication of various PPAs of the Municipality and on the timely submission of required reports to COA.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Assets Inventory Committee should
fast track the completion of the reconciliation of the physical inventory count and
accounting records, and furnish the Audit Team with the said report. The Municipal
Mayor should assign additional manpower to the General Services Officer’s Office to
expedite the reconciliation process, undergo the actual physical count, maintain
inventory cards for each item of PPE, prepare the required annual physical inventory
report and monitor the same.

We recommended the GSO to maintain property cards and establish a database
of all PPEs of the Municipality to facilitate the reconciliation of the same to the physical
inventory count.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management immediately take the
necessary actions to transfer the titles of those lands that are still under the name of its
previous owners in the name of the Municipality of Bataraza.

We recommended the Management to require its Accountable Officers to
liquidate the cash advances, through submission of the necessary supporting documents
and refund any excess amount, as the case may be.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to send demand letters to the
officials and employees with unliquidated accounts and cause the withholding of their
respective salaries, and see to it that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of
each year.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to properly maintain the books and
supporting schedules with complete details of all cash advances and require also strict
adherence thereto by bookkeepers.

Further, we recommended the Management to strictly comply with the provisions
of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and Section 89 of Presidential
Decree No. 1445 on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended the Management to insure all government properties with the
Property Insurance Fund of the GSIS.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require from the concerned officers and employees who are still in service the immediate liquidation of their cash advances totaling ₱9,378,986.49, else suspension of salaries shall be imposed until the same have been fully settled and liquidated. The cash advance for confidential/intelligence fund amounting to ₱9,760,332.44 shall remain in the books pending receipt of related Credit Notice from the Office of the COA Chairman.

Henceforth, the Municipal Mayor should strictly enforce the rules and regulations on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances prescribed in COA Circular No. 97-002 particularly the ruling which requires that no additional cash advance shall be granted to official and employees with previous unliquidated cash advance. Moreover, clearance for money and property accountability should not be issued to officials and employees who are due for transfers/separation/retirement unless they have first settled their unliquidated cash advances.

Failure to do so will make the approving authority jointly and solidarily liable for the amount cleared as provided for under Section 104 of PD 1445.

We recommended that the release/allocation of the confidential fund and/or intelligence fund and the grant of cash advances thereof be made in accordance with COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2016-01.

We recommended for the Management to explain and submit pertinent documents, if applicable, that warrant the; 
a. Payment of ₱11,753,748.69 in excess of the authorized appropriations provided in the Annual/Supplemental Budgets for Security Services; and 
b. Payment to Sidekick Force Investigation and Security Services, Inc., for the security services rendered prior to bidding.

Transactions found to be irregular/illegal shall be disallowed in audit. Henceforth, ensure that all payments/disbursements should be made strictly in pursuance of available appropriations and that procurement process prescribed in RA 9184 shall be strictly adhered to.

We further recommended that the Management amend the contract agreement with Sidekick Force Investigation and Security Services, Inc. particularly the contract period taking into consideration the 12-month period and the issuance date of the Notice of Award.
observations.reason:
Transactions found to be irregular/illegal shall be disallowed in audit. Henceforth, ensure that all payments/disbursements should be made strictly in pursuance of available appropriations and that procurement process prescribed in RA 9184 shall be strictly adhered to.

We further recommended that the Management amend the contract agreement with Sidekick Force Investigation and Security Services, Inc. particularly the contract period taking into consideration the 12-month period and the issuance date of the Notice of Award.

observations.recommendation:
5.a The Inter-LGU Alliance was entered into without conducting public hearings as in Article 61(d))(4) of the IRR of RA 7160, thereby rendering the MOA unenforceable.
5.b Four (4) alliance projects totalling ₱14 million were not started or implemented during the year despite full remittance of the municipal counterpart to PGP, thereby casting doubt on the readiness of the PGP to undertake the projects.
5.c The MOA does not contain penal provision or sanction on the non-compliance with the stipulated schedule of project implementation to ensure the timely delivery of basic services and facilitates to the constituents of the Municipality.
5.d The municipal counterpart of ₱14 million was not deposited in a joint current account contrary to Section 3.01 of MOA.
5.e Absence of documents such as feasibility studies and detailed engineering outputs, among others, which are deemed counterparts to the alliance project, casts doubts on the readiness of the PGP to undertake the alliance projects and its compliance with pertinent provisions of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
5.f Absence of documents providing significant details pertinent to project descriptions, components and implementation plan for the alliance projects which form part of the duly signed MOA.
5.g Changes in project cost shall not require amendment to MOA contrary to Article 61 (d)(4) of the IRR of RA No. 7160.
5.h There was no specific provision on the ownership of the alliance projects in the MOA.
observations.reason:
We recommended compliance as required with Article 61 of the IRR of RA No. 7160 on the conduct of public hearing by the Sangguniang Bayan. If public hearing was conducted, submit to the audit team the pertinent journal of proceedings.

We recommended for the Municipality to coordinate with the Provincial Government the execution and completion of the four (4) alliance projects, provided that the required public hearing/s was conducted. Otherwise, no subsequent transfer of municipal counterpart fund shall be made to PGP for the remaining alliance projects. Secure from the PGP the Statement of Receipts and Expenditures (SRE) for the fund transfer made as of December 31, 2016.

We recommended the inclusion pf penal provisions or sanctions in the MOA in case of non-compliance with the project implementation schedule subject to the ratification of the Sangguniang Bayan and Sangguniang Panlalawigan.

We recommended for the Municipality to ensure that the municipal counterpart fund shall be deposited in a joint bank account for the Inter-LGU alliance projects in accordance with the Section 3.01 of the MOA. Interest Income earned should be shared by both contracting parties. Submit authenticated copy of SB Resolution authorizing the LCE and the Treasurer to open a joint current account for the Inter-LGU alliance.

We recommended the submission of authenticated copies of the PGP feasibility studies, business plans and all detailed engineering outputs/documents mentioned in Annex A of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 as reference in audit.

We recommended the submission of authenticated copies of Schedules No. 1-6 forming part of the MOA including authenticated copies of resolutions passed by the Sangguniang Bayan and the Sangguniang Panlalawigan ratifying the MOA.

We recommended that all modifications or amendments to the MOA, including changes in project cost, shall require ratification by the Sangguniang Bayan and Sangguniang Panlalawigan.

We recommended the inclusion in the MOA of provision on ownership of alliance projects for proper recording in the books.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended for the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balance of the Disaster Risk, Response and Rescue Equipment account and all other affected accounts as of December 31, 2016.

We recommended that the fund used for administrative expenses of LDRRMO which should have been appropriately charged against the General Fund totaling ₱1,625,050.10 be refunded/ returned accordingly to the DRRM Fund (DRRMF), otherwise, such will be subject of audit disallowance. The MDRRMO and the Municipal Budget Officer should henceforth strictly comply with the provisions of NDRRMC-DLIG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 on the charges and utilization of DRRM fund.

We recommended that all supplies, materials, relief goods including small items purchased for disaster response and rescue activities be recorded under appropriate inventory account held for consumption or distribution and issuance thereof be supported with the corresponding Requisition and Issue Slip.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Treasurer to strictly deposit intact all her collections daily or not later than the next banking day and discontinue disbursements out of collections.

The Municipal Treasurer should also submit an explanation on the practice of partial deposit or retaining part of collections as standby fund as well as the cause of the excess deposit.

The report of Collections and Deposits prescribed by NGAS Manual should also be used by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer in reporting for its daily collections, deposits and accountable forms.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure strict adherence to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 in the preparation of Annual GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) and Accomplishment Report (AR) and the submission thereof to concerned agencies. We also recommend for the GFPS to ensure that the Annual GAD Plan and Budget was approved by the DILG before incorporating the same in the Annual Budget. The audit team should also be furnished with the GPB and AR as references in audit.

Compliance with the required GAD allocation of at least five percent (5%) of the annual budget appropriations is likewise recommended pursuant to DBM-NEDA-NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-1.

We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to require all involved municipal officials and employees to render justification on the irregularities and fraudulent acts noted in various reimbursement claims and file appropriate administrative charges against erring officials and employees. Transactions involving irregular expenditures shall be disallowed in audit.

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to immediately impose the collection of liquidated damages totaling ₱2,118,349.13 and forfeiture of related performance security, else, such shall be subject of audit charge.

Disqualification of erring contractors from participating in any public bidding is likewise recommended.

Henceforth, other applicable sanctions mentioned in Item 8, Annex “E” of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 should be strictly imposed on erring contractors in case of delay in work completion.

NEDA- Approved Constructors Performance Evaluation System (CPES) should also be prepared and submitted by the Municipal Engineer to evaluate performance of contractors for infrastructure projects.

The Municipal Accountant is likewise reminded to ensure that, prior to final payment, contractors who incurred delays should be immediately deducted liquidated damages from any money due under the contract or from the retention money or other securities posted by the erring contractor.
